name: Other soy product (food medium)
basis: per 100 g (representative full-fat soy product / soy flour, non-fermented; USDA FDC 174273 as quantitative anchor)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Moisture of full-fat soy flour ~5-7%; low because it is a dried product.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Protein is the dominant macronutrient of soy products.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 174273 (total sugars 7.5 g) apportioned by soybean sugar profile in Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453
components.evidence: Sucrose is the dominant free sugar of soy (seed range 2.5-8.2%); primary readily fermentable disaccharide.

components.name: stachyose
components.source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (soybean seed 1.4-4.1%, up to 28.2 mg/g)
components.evidence: Alpha-galactoside tetrasaccharide; a major soy oligosaccharide, fermentable by alpha-galactosidase-positive microbes. Midpoint of published range.

components.name: raffinose
components.source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (soybean seed 0.1-1.0%, up to 9.3 mg/g)
components.evidence: Alpha-galactoside trisaccharide; second soy oligosaccharide. Midpoint of published range.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (~6 mg/g max in seed)

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (~2.7 mg/g max in seed)
components.evidence: Minor free monosaccharide; already in current list, now quantified.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Jang et al. 2021 PMC8468453 (1.4-2.5 mg/g in seed)
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide, also released from alpha-galactosides.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Present only as a trace/starch-hydrolysis product in soy; retained from current list but soy has minimal free maltose.

components.name: starch
components.source: Mature soybean composition (starch typically 1-3%); note USDA carbohydrate-by-difference overstates starch
components.evidence: Mature soy seed is a low-starch legume; the bulk of non-sugar carbohydrate is fiber and oligosaccharides, not starch.

components.name: phytic acid
components.source: Soybean seed phytate 22.91 mg/g (soy composition literature; typical soybean phytate 1.0-2.3%)
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid of soy seed; strong mineral chelator (binds Ca, Fe, Zn, Mg) - relevant to mineral availability in a growth medium.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Soybean cultivar survey 82.3-213.6 mg/100g (SNI Global oxalate review); midpoint
components.evidence: Genuine soy organic acid; range 82-214 mg/100g across cultivars, one study up to 370 mg/100g total oxalate.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Kim et al. 2020 fppn doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 (1H NMR, vegetable soybean)
components.evidence: Reported as the MAIN free organic acid of soybean (malic > citric > fumaric > succinic); mature-seed absolute quantitation sparse, hence null amount with qualitative rank.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: Second most abundant free organic acid in soybean after malic; present, unquantified in mature seed.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-derived amino acid profile of soy.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Protein-derived (glutamic acid); most abundant soy amino acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-derived; CORRECTS current 'D-Serine' (proteinogenic serine is the L enantiomer).

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Trace mineral (Fe); consolidates current Fe2+/Fe3+/Iron entries into one measured value.

components.name: molybdenum
components.source: FooDB FOOD00271 / soy is a recognized high-molybdenum legume
components.evidence: Genuine trace element (cofactor for nitrate reductase etc.); present, unquantified here. Retained as 'Molybdate'.

components.name: nickel
components.evidence: Genuine trace element; present, unquantified. Retained.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Trace element; present at very low levels. Retained as 'Co2+'.

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence: Essentially absent in the processed soy product (USDA reports 0; raw mature seed ~6 mg). Retained from current 'L-Ascorbate' but corrected to near-zero.

components.name: vitamin A (as beta-carotene)
components.evidence: Provitamin-A carotenoids only; no preformed retinol in this plant food.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Fat-soluble; soy oil also rich in gamma-tocopherol.

components.name: gamma-tocopherol
components.source: FooDB FOOD00271 / soy oil tocopherol profile (gamma is the dominant soy tocopherol)
components.evidence: Genuine major soy tocopherol; present, unquantified here. Retained.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine soy osmolyte/methyl donor; present, unquantified. Retained.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant soy fatty acid; PUFA. Retained as '(Z,Z)-9,12-Octadecadienoic acid'.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Main soy monounsaturated fatty acid; ADDED.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.source: USDA FDC 174273 (saturated 2.99 g; palmitic is the major soy SFA)
components.evidence: Main soy saturated fatty acid; ADDED.

components.name: stearic acid (18:0)
components.evidence: Minor soy saturated fatty acid; ADDED.

remove.name: Eicosapentaenoic acid
remove.reason: EPA (20:5 n-3) is a marine/animal long-chain omega-3; soy oil contains NO EPA (only 18:2 and 18:3). FooDB predicted/implausible for a plant food.

remove.name: Heptanoate
remove.reason: Heptanoic acid (C7 odd-chain) is not a soy nutrient; FooDB noise, not a real growth substrate of soy.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed vitamin A (animal form) is absent in soy; the food supplies provitamin-A carotenoids (beta-carotene) only. Replaced by 'vitamin A (as beta-carotene)'.

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: Vitamin B12 is absent in non-fermented plant soy (USDA B12 = 0); synthetic cyanocobalamin is not a native soy compound. Would only apply to fermented soy (natto/tempeh) or fortified products.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Proteinogenic serine is the L enantiomer; 'D-Serine' is a database enantiomer artifact. Replaced by 'L-serine'.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
remove.reason: Redundant oxidation-state/compartment splits of one measured element; consolidated into a single 'iron' entry (6.4 mg/100g).

missing_added: sucrose, stachyose, raffinose, glucose, fructose, galactose (individual soy sugars with amounts; oligosaccharides stachyose+raffinose are the defining soy carbohydrates)
phytic acid (dominant soy organic acid / mineral chelator)
oxalic acid, malic acid, citric acid, succinic acid, fumaric acid (soy organic acids)
L-valine and L-phenylalanine (missing essential amino acids)
magnesium, phosphorus, potassium, sodium, zinc (quantified minerals absent as numbers before)
thiamine, riboflavin, niacin, B6, folate, vitamin E, vitamin K, choline amounts (quantified)
oleic acid, palmitic acid, stearic acid (fatty acid detail)
keep_uncovered: delta-tocopherol (genuine soy tocopherol; no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 174273 (Soy flour, full-fat, raw) - full macro/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
Jang et al. 2021, Foods, PMC8468453 - individual free sugars (sucrose/stachyose/raffinose/glucose/fructose/galactose) and isoflavones of soybean
Kim et al. 2020, Food Prod Process Nutr, doi:10.1186/s43014-020-00021-1 - soybean organic-acid profile (malic dominant)
SNI Global oxalate review / soybean cultivar survey - oxalate 82.3-213.6 mg/100g
Soybean phytate composition literature - phytic acid ~2.3 g/100g
notes: 'Other soy product' is a generic FooDB category with no single defining recipe; quantified here against full-fat soy flour (USDA FDC 174273) as a representative non-fermented soy product. Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe are the free/oligo sugars - sucrose (~4.5 g) plus the alpha-galactosides stachyose (~2.5 g) and raffinose (~0.8 g) - together with abundant amino acids from ~38% protein. Phytic acid (~2.3 g) is the dominant organic acid and a strong mineral chelator affecting Ca/Fe/Zn/Mg availability. If the actual product is FERMENTED soy (tempeh/natto/miso/soy sauce), add lactic and acetic acid as major organic acids and non-zero vitamin B12, and reduce oligosaccharides (fermented out).
